document.title: M1 Lattice: Semantic Examination
document.purpose: Describes the conceptual roles of common entity fields, framing them as dimensions of meaning such as identity, classification, explanation, history, and attention.

overview: An entity in the M1 Lattice is not just a collection of fields, but a structured presence that can be understood along multiple semantic dimensions. These dimensions help guide both human understanding and system design.

semantic_dimensions.description: What the entity is, in a stable and referential sense. Identity is persistent and does not change over time.
How the entity is grouped or categorized. Classification is flexible and may evolve.
What the entity means. Provides context, interpretation, and understanding.
What has happened to the entity over time. Captures events, changes, and provenance.
What draws attention to the entity. A concise, engaging expression that invites interaction or conveys significance quickly.

notes: These dimensions are not required fields, but lenses through which entities can be understood.
Multiple fields or structures may contribute to a single semantic dimension.
Not all entities must express all dimensions.
These dimensions support both human cognition and system design.
